fandac_air_quality_address_policy.note: Address-precise refinement of the fandac_conflicts_with_airiq mutex (ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-CONFIGS-001). The mutex boolean above stays TRUE and is NOT relaxed: it keeps AirIQ+FanDAC out of the one-click WebFlash firmware-combination grammar, which cannot set the FanDAC DIP switch. This object documents the underlying hardware-address reason and the manual exception.
fandac_air_quality_address_policy.rationale: The S360-312 FanDAC board carries two GP8403 DACs (package defaults IC1 @ 0x58, IC2 @ 0x59). Both air-quality modules (AirIQ S360-210 and VentIQ S360-211) carry an SGP41 VOC/NOx sensor at I2C 0x59 on the shared core_i2c bus, so the FanDAC default IC2 @ 0x59 collides with the SGP41.
fandac_air_quality_address_policy.precise_rule: AirIQ/VentIQ conflict with FanDAC ONLY when the FanDAC GP8403 uses 0x59. With IC2 relocated to 0x5A (firmware override fan_dac_2_i2c_address plus the matching IC2 DIP switch), AirIQ/VentIQ + FanDAC can coexist.
fandac_air_quality_address_policy.webflash_one_click_posture: Not exposed in WebFlash one-click: the one-click surface cannot set the DIP switch, so AirIQ+FanDAC stays out of the grammar (fandac_conflicts_with_airiq). The address-overridden AirIQ/VentIQ + FanDAC room-bundle configs are advanced/manual previews only (config/room-bundle-fan-variants.json fan_dac_i2c_address_policy).
